content: Lithium prices collapsed 80% in 2023-24 from peak levels due to faster-than-expected supply ramp-up and EV demand slowdown. New mines in Australia and brine operations in Chile came online ahead of schedule. Sodium-ion batteries emerged as viable alternatives for stationary storage and lower-range EVs, potentially reducing long-term lithium intensity. Recycling could meet 30% of demand by 2035, further pressuring primary supply.
